proposed.title: Ghedi Ghedi
proposed.summary: A resident DJ at Beneath the Baobabs Festival - where he's helped to shape the curation of the main stages and ring in New Year celebrations with his signature sound.

proposed.title: BBYY.ontheisland
proposed.summary: A music producer, DJ, and writer based in Mombasa, known for crafting hypnotic, emotionally rich soundscapes that blend experimental textures with East African rhythm and global club culture.

proposed.title: Heka Heka
proposed.summary: The sonic embodiment of ancient mysticism, crafting music that is both hypnotic and psychedelic. His sound is a journey through rhythm and consciousness, fusing pulsating tribal beats with ethereal melodies and mind-bending textures to create immersive auditory rituals.

proposed.title: Soulfud
proposed.summary: A passionate and vibrant DJ from Kilifi, Kenya. In just over two years behind the decks, she has built a strong reputation across East Africa’s underground scene for her eclectic sound and magnetic stage presence by blending electronic funk, house, melodic techno, afrohouse, afro-tech, and afrocentric sounds.

proposed.title: Shangatatu
proposed.summary: A multi-hyphenate brand featuring the pioneer- Ian Luganje Ziro, his art and merchandise creations, lastly, Ian’s musical interpretation in Dj format and music production. Shangatatu is a vibrant creative born and rooted on Kenya's coast channeling positive frequencies through African dance music, merchandise and art into hypnotic, tribal soundscapes that speak to both ancient rhythm and modern freedom.

proposed.title: Lutsaga Project
proposed.summary: A funded project of social responsibility, Lutsaga pays all participants for their performances, ensures royalties are distributed fairly, and returns master recordings to the communities themselves. Sustainability Beneath the Baobabs Festival proudly bans all single-use plastics, and we encourage everyone to join us in eliminating these pollutants from our everyday lives.

proposed.summary: A theatre event and art exhibition in Nairobi.
